page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8481C cores running at 2.7 GHz, with 2 threads per core. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. The CPU architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning and matrix operations.

Benchmark results show a divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core execution is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1620.59 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1635.0, both falling within the average range. However, multi-core performance is constrained, as evidenced by a multi-core Geekbench score of 3703.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 3324.57, both categorized in the bottom 25%. Memory subsystem performance is a notable bottleneck; the Passmark memory score of 989.87 is in the bottom 10%, and uncached large-block memory read bandwidth is limited to 27.54 GB/sec.

This hardware profile is suited for workloads that rely on single-threaded execution or specialized instruction sets rather than high memory throughput or massive parallelization. It demonstrates average performance in static web serving (6.78 GB/sec) and small-scale LLM inference, processing 393.48 tokens per second for a 135M parameter model. However, it is less effective for memory-intensive database operations, software compilation, or large-scale multi-threaded processing. The qualitative cost efficiency of the c3-highcpu-4 is defined by its low-memory, compute-focused allocation, making it a budget-conscious option for compute tasks that do not require substantial memory footprints.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate a clear division: single-core capabilities are average, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1635.0, whereas multi-core and memory-bound performance are weak, highlighted by a Passmark memory score in the bottom 10%. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX, which assist in specialized tasks such as small-scale LLM inference. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-focused workloads with low memory requirements, such as static web serving and single-threaded processing, but it is poorly suited for memory-intensive databases or complex multi-threaded compilations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, offering average single-core performance but limited memory bandwidth.

tagline: A compute-focused virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors with advanced instruction support for low-memory, single-threaded, and web-serving workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8481C at 2.7 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory with 2.0 GB per core ratio
Supports Intel AVX-512 and AMX advanced instruction sets
Average single-core performance but constrained memory bandwidth
Appropriate for static web serving and small-scale LLM inference
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
